fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: District of Columbia CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to District of Columbia - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity district of columbia, how to transfer CNA license to district of columbia, district of columbia CNA endorsement application

board.name: DC Board of Nursing
board.mailing_address: 899 North Capitol Street, NE, 1st Floor, Washington, DC 20002

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in District of Columbia.

steps.title: Complete an Approved Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a Nursing Assistant training and competency evaluation program approved by the District of Columbia Board of Nursing.

steps.title: Create Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register for an account on the DC Health Licensing Portal (https://doh.force.com/dchealth) and complete the Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) application.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or mail all necessary documents as instructed within the DC Health Licensing Portal, including official transcripts/completion certificates from your training program, a 2x2 passport-size photo, and photo identification.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions within the online application to complete the required criminal background check.

steps.title: Register for and Pass the Competency Exam
steps.description: Once your application is approved by the Board of Nursing, register with Prometric (https://www.prometric.com/nursing-aides/dc) to schedule and pass the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) competency examination.

steps.title: Receive Certification
steps.description: Upon successful completion of the competency examination, Prometric will notify the DC Board of Nursing, and your name will be added to the DC Nurse Aide Registry.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does District of Columbia CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $15. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in District of Columbia.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 6 to 8 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in District of Columbia while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in DC.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to District of Columbia.

hero.title: Become a CNA in District of Columbia
hero.dek: Applicants for Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) licensure in the District of Columbia must apply online through the DC Health Licensing Portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in District of Columbia
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to District of Columbia CNA certification.

credential_type.registry_name: District of Columbia Nurse Aide Registry

official_links.registration_steps: New users must click 'Register for an Account' on the portal homepage, create a username and password, and follow the prompts to complete the registration process before starting an application.
